summary_en: 19,872 million yen allocated to acquire Sompo Holdings shares via open market (Jun 1 - Aug 31, 2026) for a new J-ESOP equity compensation plan covering approximately 48,000 employees across Sompo and 30+ domestic subsidiaries. Annual allotment of 20 points per employee converts to 20 shares upon vesting; shares held in trust and distributed to eligible beneficiaries per stock grant regulations.
title_en: Notice on Introduction of Group Employee Stock Compensation Plan and Treasury Share Acquisition under J-ESOP (Stock Grant Trust)
translation_en_brief:
On May 20, 2026, Sompo Holdings resolved to establish a J-ESOP (stock grant trust) equity compensation program for employees of Sompo and more than 30 domestic group subsidiaries, covering approximately 48,000 eligible participants. To support this program, the company will acquire its own shares valued at 19,872 million yen via open-market purchases from June 1 through August 31, 2026.

Under the scheme, participating employees receive annual point allocations (initially 20 points per year). Upon satisfying prescribed vesting conditions based on tenure, employees become eligible to receive shares equivalent to their accumulated points (initially converting to 20 shares). All shares to be distributed will be acquired in advance and held in trust by Mizuho Trust Bank (with Japan Custody Bank as sub-trustee), managed separately as trust assets. A trust manager selected from Sompo employees will direct voting rights.

The program aims to heighten employee awareness of enterprise value, foster cohesion across the Sompo group, and contribute to employee financial wellbeing.
